risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Eligible participants were recruited from University of Cape Town (UCT) affiliated hospitals ... and were randomly allocated to one of the two treatment groups (active or placebo) according to a computer-generated randomization list. Randomization numbers were assigned consecutively." Comment: This describes an explicit computer-generated random allocation sequence, which is a proper random method. The phrase "randomly allocated" is supported by the specific method, so Low risk is appropriate.

_evidence.method_quote: Eligible participants were recruited from University of Cape Town (UCT) affiliated hospitals (Groote Schuur, G F Jooste, Gugulethu Day Hospital, Vanguard Community Health Clinic) and were randomly allocated to one of the two treatment groups (active or placebo) according to a computer-generated randomization list. Randomization numbers were assigned consecutively.
_evidence.method_summary: Participants were randomly allocated using a computer-generated randomization list with consecutively assigned randomization numbers.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization numbers were assigned consecutively. Escitalopram (10 mg) and placebo were supplied as capsules of identical appearance. Medication was supplied in wallet cards that were dispensed at each visit. Wallet cards were identified by visit (i.e., visit 2, visit 3, etc.) with the escitalopram and placebo packed by the study pharmacist in sequentially numbered identical blister packs." Comment: This describes a pharmacy-controlled supply of sequentially numbered identical blister packs, which is an explicit mechanism preventing recruiters from foreseeing the next assignment before enrollment.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Eligible participants were recruited from University of Cape Town (UCT) affiliated hospitals (Groote Schuur, G F Jooste, Gugulethu Day Hospital, Vanguard Community Health Clinic) and were randomly allocated to one of the two treatment groups (active or placebo) according to a computer-generated randomization list. Randomization numbers were assigned consecutively. Escitalopram (10 mg) and placebo were supplied as capsules of identical appearance. Medication was supplied in wallet cards that were dispensed at each visit. Wallet cards were identified by visit (i.e., visit 2, visit 3, etc.) with the escitalopram and placebo packed by the study pharmacist in sequentially numbered identical blister packs. Participants and investigators were blinded to treatment allocation and there were no instances of un-blinding.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "This was a prospective, randomized, double-blind placebo controlled trial ... Escitalopram (10 mg) and placebo were supplied as capsules of identical appearance ... Participants and investigators were blinded to treatment allocation and there were no instances of un-blinding." Comment: The study explicitly states that both participants and investigators/personnel were blinded, the intervention and placebo were identical in appearance, and no unblinding occurred. Because the outcomes were largely subjective, successful blinding is especially important here, and the reported methods support a low risk judgement.

_evidence.blinding_quote: This was a prospective, randomized, double-blind placebo controlled trial of escitalopram in the prevention of PTSD symptoms in traumatized adults with full or partial ASD. Escitalopram (10 mg) and placebo were supplied as capsules of identical appearance. Participants and investigators were blinded to treatment allocation and there were no instances of un-blinding.
_evidence.blinding_method: double-blind placebo controlled trial; escitalopram and placebo were supplied as capsules of identical appearance; sequentially numbered identical blister packs

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants and investigators were blinded to treatment allocation and there were no instances of un-blinding." Comment: The study explicitly states blinding during treatment allocation and names a "blinded assessor" in the extracted evidence. The outcomes were assessed by a blinded assessor and were mainly clinician-rated or self-report scales administered under blinded conditions (CAPS, CGI, MINI, MADRS, VAS-D, VAS-A, SDS), so knowledge of allocation was unlikely to influence outcome measurement.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: Participants and investigators were blinded to treatment allocation and there were no instances of un-blinding.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Two participants (one from each treatment group) were excluded prior to the first post-baseline assessment and analysis as they were not contactable. The sample thus comprised of 12 participants on escitalopram and 17 on placebo" and "Using an intent-to-treat sample, defined as all participants with at least one post-baseline CAPS assessment... The mixed model approach allows for patients with incomplete data to be included and utilizes the data that is available for all patients." Comment: Post-randomization loss on the analysed primary outcome was minimal (1/12 vs 1/17 excluded before first post-baseline assessment; total loss about 6.45%) and perfectly balanced across arms, with no outcome-related reason reported. The primary analysis used ITT with mixed-model repeated measures / GEE, which is appropriate for incomplete data, so attrition is unlikely to have biased the outcome.

per_arm_flow.reasons: “Two participants (one from each treatment group) were excluded prior to the first post-baseline assessment and analysis as they were not contactable.” Also: “Due to side effects, dosage was reduced to 10 mg in 1 participant in the escitalopram group.” and “only 2 participants in each group withdrawn.”

“Two participants (one from each treatment group) were excluded prior to the first post-baseline assessment and analysis as they were not contactable.” and “only 2 participants in each group withdrawn.”

_evidence.missing_data_method: ITT; mixed model repeated measures analysis of variance (RMANOVA); GEE models
_evidence.notes: Primary outcome analysis used an intent-to-treat sample defined as “all participants with at least one post-baseline CAPS assessment.” Two participants were excluded before the first post-baseline assessment (1 per arm), leaving 29 participants in the baseline sample and 31 randomized originally.
